Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration
Off Calendar May-01-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RESPONDENT SEPHORA USA, INC.'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION

Appearances via Zoom:
Kiran Bhat of Keller Postman LLC (202.918.1123, kiran.bhat@kellerpostman.com) for petitioner Michelle Harrison; No other appearances.

This matter is not argued. Mr. Bhat indicates that counsel are being directed by the clerk's office to adhere to the practice of noticing hearings for even numbered cases in department 302. The court confirms that department 301 is unofficially hearing the 29 related Sephora cases and will make adjustments to the calendar appropriately.

The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: The court sua sponte grants reconsideration and vacates its order of March 13, 2026 granting petitioner's motion to compel arbitration. Sephora has leave to file a supplemental brief of no more than five pages concerning the issues of petitioner's actual or constructive notice of any relevant Beauty Insider Terms and Conditions discussed in the court's now-vacated March 13 order. Sephora shall file its brief by May 7, 2026. Petitioner may file a supplemental brief in response of no more than five pages by May 13, 2026. The petition is set for further hearing on May 19, 2026. Sephora's motion for reconsideration is denied as moot. Proposed order submitted. Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Shannon Borge, CSR 9436 (916.320.1010, shannonborgecsr@gmail.com) (301/CVA)
